title: Hard rename knowledge_language → fabric_language + add zh-CN-hybrid enum value (schema + types + tests + dogfood config)
description: Foundational schema change: rename Zod field knowledge_language → fabric_language with NO z.preprocess alias (hard rename per user-confirmed clean-slate). Add a new enum value 'zh-CN-hybrid' (Chinese narrative prose with English technical terms preserved) alongside the existing 'match-existing' | 'zh-CN' | 'en'. Rename the exported Zod schema constant knowledgeLanguageSchema → fabricLanguageSchema. Update the TS type alias KnowledgeLanguage → FabricLanguage and the interface field FabricConfig.knowledge_language → FabricConfig.fabric_language. Update the unit tests in packages/shared/test/fabric-config.test.ts (~30+ references). Update the dogfood config .fabric/fabric-config.json. Update related docs.

files.target: L27 schema declaration + L45 field name
files.change: Rename const `knowledgeLanguageSchema` → `fabricLanguageSchema`. Add 'zh-CN-hybrid' to the z.enum literal so it becomes z.enum(['match-existing', 'zh-CN', 'en', 'zh-CN-hybrid']). Rename the field on fabricConfigSchema (L45) from `knowledge_language: knowledgeLanguageSchema.optional().default('match-existing')` to `fabric_language: fabricLanguageSchema.optional().default('match-existing')`. Update the export statement. NO z.preprocess fallback for the old key — hard rename.

files.target: L17 type alias + L29 interface field
files.change: Rename `type KnowledgeLanguage = 'match-existing' | 'zh-CN' | 'en'` → `type FabricLanguage = 'match-existing' | 'zh-CN' | 'en' | 'zh-CN-hybrid'`. Rename interface field `knowledge_language?: KnowledgeLanguage` → `fabric_language?: FabricLanguage`. Update any default export statements.

Rename all references to knowledgeLanguageSchema → fabricLanguageSchema and to the field knowledge_language → fabric_language. Add new test case for the 'zh-CN-hybrid' enum value (valid value, parses successfully, type-narrows correctly). Update describe block title 'knowledge_language' → 'fabric_language'. Verify default behavior still resolves to 'match-existing'.

Rename all JSON-key references in test fixtures from knowledge_language to fabric_language. Rename describe block titles (knowledge_language_en_produces_english_baselines etc.) and assertions (parsed.knowledge_language → parsed.fabric_language). Update userSeed.knowledge_language → userSeed.fabric_language. Note: scan-init.test.ts integration tests will fail until both schema AND scan.ts runtime read sites are renamed in lockstep — coordinate with TASK-005.

Rename JSON key from "knowledge_language": "zh-CN" to "fabric_language": "zh-CN-hybrid" (the project itself is the canonical case for the new hybrid mode — CJK README + English Skill protected tokens). Note: this also implicitly tests TASK-005 detectExistingLanguage CJK-signal path.

Rename heading '### knowledge_language (default match-existing)' → '### fabric_language (default match-existing)'. Update body description text. Document the new 'zh-CN-hybrid' value with its rendering rule: 'Chinese narrative prose with English technical terms preserved (MCP tool names, CLI commands, file paths, technical concepts).' Document the 'fabric install' verb change (replaces 'fabric init').

Rename schema reference from 'knowledgeLanguageSchema' → 'fabricLanguageSchema'. Rename field references 'knowledge_language' → 'fabric_language'. Add the new 'zh-CN-hybrid' value to the enum documentation.
Replace 'knowledge_language config field' with 'fabric_language config field' in the layer-language-detection note.

Leave untouched per memory:feedback_clean_slate — pending knowledge entries capture past decisions; renaming would distort historical record. The pending entry's title and body remain references to the old name as design history.

implementation: Edit packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts: rename const + field key + add 'zh-CN-hybrid' enum value; verify the export statement matches the new name; remove any back-compat preprocessor
Edit packages/shared/src/types/config.ts: rename type alias + interface field; verify no z.preprocess remnants
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build` to verify TS types compile

Update packages/shared/test/fabric-config.test.ts: find-replace all schema-name + field-name references; add positive test for 'zh-CN-hybrid' enum value parsing; add negative test for old 'knowledge_language' key being rejected (or silently ignored — confirm Zod behavior)
Update packages/cli/__tests__/integration/scan-init.test.ts: rename all knowledge_language → fabric_language in test fixtures and assertions
Update .fabric/fabric-config.json: rename JSON key and switch value from 'zh-CN' → 'zh-CN-hybrid' (this project's dogfood is the canonical hybrid-mode reference)
Update docs/configuration.md heading + body; add zh-CN-hybrid documentation
Update docs/data-schema.md schema-export reference and field reference
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared test` and verify fabric-config.test.ts passes

Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli typecheck` and verify no TS errors from downstream consumers (scan.ts, install.ts will fail until TASK-005 + TASK-002 land — expected; commit this task in isolation may break those files temporarily until TASK-005/002 are merged)

reference.pattern: Zod schema rename pattern: Zod export → TS type alias → interface field → test references → docs. The export at L27 of fabric-config.ts is the single source of truth; all other names are mechanical mirrors.
reference.examples: No prior precedent in this codebase for a Zod field rename; closest reference is the original introduction of knowledgeLanguageSchema in rc.5.

criteria: Grep for 'knowledge_language' (snake_case JSON key) across packages/shared/src + packages/shared/test + .fabric/fabric-config.json + docs/configuration.md + docs/data-schema.md + docs/mcp-contracts.md returns zero results (historical pending entry .fabric/knowledge/pending/decisions/init-fixate-knowledge-language.md excepted)
Grep for 'KnowledgeLanguage' (TS type) returns zero results
fabricLanguageSchema and FabricLanguage are exported and importable
z.enum literal includes 'zh-CN-hybrid' as a valid value
Positive test case for 'zh-CN-hybrid' value exists in fabric-config.test.ts

rationale.chosen_approach: Hard rename with no Zod preprocess fallback. The new 'zh-CN-hybrid' enum value enables the technical-term-preservation rendering rule that TASK-004 codifies in Skill prompts. Dogfood config update to 'zh-CN-hybrid' makes this project the canonical reference implementation of the new mode.
alternatives_considered: z.preprocess that rewrites 'knowledge_language' key to 'fabric_language' at parse time (rejected: clean-slate, zero users)
Defer the zh-CN-hybrid addition to a future task (rejected: zh-CN-hybrid is foundational for TASK-004 Skill prompts; better to land together)
Migration codemod that rewrites user fabric-config.json files (rejected: clean-slate)
decision_factors: Brand consistency — 'fabric_language' matches the product name
Practical need for zh-CN-hybrid mode in real projects with CJK + English technical vocabulary
rationale.tradeoffs: Any future user with a pre-rc.12 fabric-config.json will fail parsing with a clear Zod error 'Unrecognized key' — acceptable for the zero-user state and the CHANGELOG entry will document it.

manual_checks: Parse .fabric/fabric-config.json with the new schema; verify it parses to {fabric_language: 'zh-CN-hybrid'}
Try parsing an old-shape config {knowledge_language: 'zh-CN'}; verify Zod rejects with clear error message
Verify TS compile catches any forgotten old-name references (build fails on any leftover .knowledge_language access)
success_metrics: All packages/shared tests pass with renamed field references
Dogfood config parses successfully with new key + new value
TypeScript compile produces zero errors in packages/shared
integration: Coordination with TASK-005 (scan.ts CJK detection returns 'zh-CN-hybrid') and TASK-004 (Skill prompts reference fabric_language)
